lasersport.org.au
Platform Guide · 2026
The competition runs on this

THE ALSA
PORTAL

The digital home of Australasian laser sport. One platform designed to run ZLTAC and other competitions end to end: player registration, teams, the rules test, legal agreements, volunteers, payments and the full committee back office.

For Players For Captains For Volunteers For Committee
Zone Laser Tag Australasian Championship lasersport.org.au
01 / OVERVIEW
What the portal is

One platform for the whole sport

ALSA Inc. is the governing body behind ZLTAC and Australasian laser sport. The portal is how the association runs it: a single, self-serve system that can replace spreadsheets, paper forms and email chains.

Run the championship

Registration, teams, side events, the rules test, payments and competition day administration, all in one place.

Build the community

Player profiles, team hubs, volunteer coordination and a public history that celebrates the sport's legends.

Govern the sport

Code of Conduct, media consent and minor approvals captured properly, with an audit trail the committee can stand behind.

Built so non-coders can run it

Content, documents, pricing and event dates can all be managed from the front end by committee members, without needing a developer or touching the database. Upload a new Code of Conduct, open registrations, set the pass mark on the rules test, all from the admin panel.

  • No spreadsheets. Live registration data, always current.
  • No paper forms. Agreements signed and stored digitally.
  • No chasing. Players see exactly what they still owe and need to do.
Two kinds of membership
FREEPortal Member

Sign up free to register for events, complete required forms, manage your profile and pay your fees.

ANNUALALSA Member

Paid annual membership of the incorporated association. Granted by the committee for each membership year, shown with an ALSA Member badge on the member's profile, with voting rights at AGMs.

ALSA Portal · Platform Guide01 · Overview
02 / ROLES
Who it serves

Five people, one portal

Everyone signs in to the same site and sees only what their role needs. Access is enforced on the server, not just hidden in the interface.

The Player

Registers for an event, joins or creates a team, signs the agreements, passes the rules test, picks side events and pays. Their Player Hub shows every step at a glance.

The Team Captain

Owns the team: creates it, names it, uploads the logo, and builds the roster by searching for registered players and adding them directly. Tracks each member's progress and submits the team for committee approval through the Captain Hub.

The Volunteer

Signs up for the roles that keep the event running. Captains can see who on their team has volunteered, with quotas tracked per event.

The Committee

Runs everything: events, documents, pricing, approvals, payments, the public history and the rules test question bank. Full admin with an audit trail.

The Competition Manager

A delegated organiser for a specific competition such as a Pre Nats event. The committee can hand a manager their own scoped dashboard to moderate team names and approvals for that competition only, without giving away the keys to everything.

ALSA Portal · Platform Guide02 · Roles
03 / EVENTS
What you can compete in

ZLTAC and beyond

The portal runs the flagship championship and the smaller competitions that lead into it, each with its own registration window, pricing and rules.

The flagship

ZLTAC

The Zone Laser Tag Australasian Championship. The premier championship of Australasian laser sport. Players travel to a different Australian or New Zealand city each year to play for the title.

The blue ribbon event is the 5 player Teams format.
The pathway

Competitions

Other events, such as Pre Nats, run through the same portal. Each is managed independently, can be handed to a delegated manager, and uses the same registration, approval and team tools. In manager facing copy these are called Competitions, not events.

The formats

Teams

The headline 5 player event. Approved, locked and rostered through the Captain Hub.

Solos

One player, all skill, no one to hide behind.

Doubles

Two player pairings, managed as partner side events.

Triples

Three player squads with partner linking and swaps.

Lord of the Rings

The much loved LotR side event.

Masters · Womens · Juniors

Masters for 35+, the Womens event, and Juniors for ages 12 to 18.

Formats are configured per event. The committee turns formats on or off, sets their price, and the player only sees what applies to the event they are registering for.

ALSA Portal · Platform Guide03 · Events
04 / PLAYER
The player journey

From sign-up to start line

Registration is a guided checklist. Completion can be gated on every required step, helping make sure no one turns up unregistered, unsigned or unpaid.

06

ALSA Portal · Platform Guide04 · Player Journey
05 / RULES TEST
The built-in referee test

Know the rules before you play

A built-in, two part knowledge test players can be required to pass before competing. The committee writes the questions; the portal scores them and gates the result.

lasersport.org.au/rules-test
Section one

Safety

Critical safety knowledge. You must score 100% to continue. Get one wrong and you retake the section.

10 questions · 100% to pass
lasersport.org.au/rules-test
Section two

General Rules

The rules and regulations of play. Pass mark is set by the committee, typically 70%.

Configurable count · 70% to pass

Safety gate

You cannot reach the general section until safety is perfect. Failed safety attempts are never logged.

Shuffled every time

Questions and answer options are reshuffled on every attempt, and drawn from a larger pool, so no two runs are the same.

Scored on the server

Answers are graded by the server against the hidden answer key, making results far harder to fake from the browser.

The whole bank is managed from the admin panel. The committee adds, edits and tags each question as Safety or General, sets how many appear, and sets the general pass mark, no developer required.

ALSA Portal · Platform Guide05 · Rules Test
06 / COMPLIANCE
Agreements done properly

Code of Conduct, Media Release & minors

The agreements that protect the players and the association, captured digitally with timestamps and tied to the specific event year.

  • View the PDF, then agree. The committee uploads each document; players open it and tick a clear consent box. The tick, with its timestamp, is the electronic signature.
  • No registering around it. Code of Conduct and Media Release can be required for everyone, with registration held until both are signed.
  • Minors handled correctly. Under 18 players download a parental consent form, a parent signs it, and the committee approves it by hand in the admin panel.
  • Versioned and year-scoped. A new version can require re-acceptance. Each year's agreements are tracked separately, so consent always matches the event being played.
lasersport.org.au/hub
Required agreements
Code of Conduct 2027.pdf
Agreed 14 Jan 2027 · 9:02pm
Media Release 2027.pdf
View PDF, then tick to agree
Under 18 Consent
Awaiting committee approval

ALSA Portal · Platform Guide06 · Compliance
07 / PAYMENTS
Money, tracked cleanly

Every dollar accounted for

The portal can track and reconcile payments without ever touching card details. Players pay by bank transfer using a reference unique to them; the committee records what lands.

lasersport.org.au/hub#payment
Amount owing
$185.00
Partial
Your payment reference
2027CROUCHY
BankALSA Inc.
BSB000-000
Account0000 0000
How it works
  • A reference that is yours alone. Built from the year and your alias, like 2027CROUCHY, so the committee can match a transfer to a player instantly.
  • A running ledger. Part payments, full payments and refunds are all recorded, each with a reason, building a clean audit trail per player.
  • Status at a glance. Unpaid, partial, paid or overpaid, shown to the player and totalled on the committee dashboard.
  • No card data, ever. The portal is display and reconcile only. There is no payment gateway and nothing sensitive to store.

When money arrives, a committee member hits Record Payment, enters the amount and the player's balance updates live.

ALSA Portal · Platform Guide07 · Payments
08 / TEAMS
For the captain

The Captain Hub

Captains run their team from one screen: create it, find registered players by search, build the roster and submit it for committee approval.

lasersport.org.au/team
1UP
1UP
ZLTAC 2027 · Roster 4 of 5
Draft
Add players
mor
MoroQLD+ Add
MorriganVIC+ Add
Roster
CROUCHYCaptain
RuntReady
WaxPayment due

Search, then add

No join codes. The captain searches players who are signed up to the portal and registered for the event, and adds them straight to the roster. The committee can add players manually in the backend when needed.

See everyone's status

The captain sees each member's readiness at a glance, so they know exactly who is holding the team up.

Submit for approval

With at least five players on the roster, the captain submits the team. It moves from draft to pending, the committee approves or rejects it in the backend, and a rejected team can fix up and re-submit. While a team is pending or approved, roster changes are locked so the draw stays stable.

Draft› Pending› Approved

Team identity comes with it: name, logo upload and a team colour picked from the shared palette.

ALSA Portal · Platform Guide08 · Teams
09 / VOLUNTEERS
The people who run the day

Volunteers, organised

A tournament this size runs on volunteers. The portal can turn a messy signup sheet into a role library, per-role approvals and per-team quotas the committee can actually enforce.

lasersport.org.au/hub#volunteering
Volunteering · ZLTAC 2027
Master Referee ✓ Approved
Commentator Pending
Technical Assistant ✗ Declined

Note: Not all volunteers will be utilised. Selection is based on the operational capacity of the ZLTAC event.

Team quotas

The committee can require volunteers per team, with a count and an enforcement mode: soft shows under-quota teams a warning at registration, hard blocks team registration completion until the quota is met.

The role library

Eleven event roles ship out of the box, from Tournament Coordinator down, each with a target count, minimum count and experience requirements. The committee edits the library from the admin panel.

Tournament Coordinator Event Coordinator Assistant EC Master Referee Media Team Director Commentator Guest Commentator Production Assistant Technical Team Manager Technical Assistant TORN Assistant
  • Approval per role. Each offered role is approved or declined individually by the committee. An approved role locks in; the player cannot silently withdraw from it.
  • Experience where it matters. Senior roles like Tournament Coordinator carry documented experience requirements, shown to players before they put their hand up.
  • One admin page runs it. Roles, event settings and signups sit in three tabs, with filters and a one-click CSV export of the volunteer list for the day.
  • Lock-aware. Signups follow the event lifecycle: existing offers lock with registration, while new offers stay open until the event closes.
ALSA Portal · Platform Guide09 · Volunteers

11 / COMPETITIONS
Beyond the flagship

Pre Nats and other competitions

Smaller competitions run through the same portal with their own lightweight flow, and can be handed to a delegated manager without giving away committee access.

lasersport.org.au/competitions
Events & competitions
ZLTAC 2027Rego Open

The Australasian Championship · Melbourne VIC

Pre Nats 2027Coming Soon

Warm-up competition · lead-in to the championship

State SeriesClosed

Local competition run by a delegated manager

A hub of their own

Players registered for a competition get a Competition Hub: create or manage a team with a name and a team colour from the shared palette, and see payment details once the manager switches them on.

Delegated managers

The committee assigns a manager to a competition. The manager gets a scoped dashboard to moderate team names and approvals for that competition only, nothing else.

Same engine underneath

Registration, teams, payment references and approvals reuse the same machinery as ZLTAC, so a competition costs the committee almost nothing to spin up.

ALSA Portal · Platform Guide11 · Competitions
12 / OPERATIONS
The safety nets

Built for the messy reality

Real tournaments have late entries, people who never made an account, and the occasional problem player. The portal has a tool for each.

Placeholder registrations

A committee member can register a player who has no portal account yet, with side events and partner pairings included. When that player eventually signs up, the portal spots the match and prompts them to claim their registration, keeping everything they were entered into.

Automated backups

Scheduled database backups run automatically and land in private object storage, with a Run Backup Now button for peace of mind before big changes. Frequency and status live on their own admin page.

Account suspension

If it ever comes to it, the committee can suspend an account: login is blocked at the authentication layer and the suspension flows through to what the account can touch. Restoring is one click.

Signed documents register

A dedicated admin panel lists every Code of Conduct and Media Release acceptance, who signed, which version, and when, so the association can answer "did this player agree?" in seconds.

Manual overrides exist for everything, Code of Conduct, media, rules test and under 18, and every override is stamped with who set it, when, and why. Flexibility without losing the audit trail.

ALSA Portal · Platform Guide12 · Operations
13 / PUBLIC
The public face

A living history of the sport

Beyond registration, the portal is the public home of ZLTAC: every championship year, every host city, the legends and the dynasties, all driven from data the committee controls.

lasersport.org.au/zltac

Search and filter every championship by decade and host state, with full placings.

Hall of Fame

Inductees and legends of the sport, edited by the committee, no code touched.

Hosts & dynasties

Where each title was won, and the teams that defined an era of the championship.

ALSA Portal · Platform Guide13 · Public
14 / FOUNDATIONS
Built to last

Under the hood

A modern, secure stack chosen so the association can run a national event reliably, and so the committee stays in control of its own data.

Secure by design

Every sensitive action is checked on the server, and the database enforces who can see and change what, row by row. Test scores are graded server side and cannot be faked from a browser.

Hosted in the region

The database and the site run from Sydney, close to the players and the committee, on infrastructure that scales for the spike of nationals registration without manual effort.

It talks to people

Confirmation and contact emails are sent from the association's own domain, with sensible limits in place so the contact form cannot be abused.

Owned by the association

Documents, photos, history and registrations all live in the association's own storage. Content is editable by the committee from the front end, with no lock-in to a developer.

The stack, in plain terms
React 19 + Vite Supabase · Sydney Vercel hosting Resend email Row level security Role based access
ALSA Portal · Platform Guide14 · Foundations
